prompt:
strength.py drives the little meter on our signup form — score() does length tiers plus character-class variety, verdict() turns that into 'very weak'..'strong'. Security review just dinged us because it happily calls 'Qwerty2024!' strong, so I got handed the upgrade ticket. Keep score()/verdict() exactly as they are (the old API is still wired to the meter and its behavior is frozen); everything new goes alongside.

Entropy: entropy_bits(pw) = len(pw) * log2(pool), rounded to 1 decimal, where the pool sums the sizes of the classes actually present: lower 26, upper 26, digits 10, and symbols 32 (anything that isn't an ASCII letter or digit is a symbol, non-ASCII included). Each class counts once no matter how often it appears; empty password is 0.0.

Patterns: find_patterns(pw) returns the distinct pattern names found, always in the canonical order sequence, repeat, keyboard, year. 'sequence' = 4+ characters in a row that are consecutive ascending or descending, case-insensitive, within the alphabet or within the digits ('abcd', '9876' — 'abc' is only 3, doesn't count). 'repeat' = the same character 3+ times in a row. 'keyboard' = 4+ characters in a row read straight off one of the rows qwertyuiop / asdfghjkl / zxcvbnm, forward or reversed, case-insensitive. 'year' = the password ENDS with 19xx or 20xx — a year in the middle is fine.

Scoring: effective_bits(pw) = entropy_bits minus 10 per pattern type found, floored at 0 and rounded to 1 decimal. entropy_score(pw) maps effective bits to 0-4: under 28 is 0, under 36 is 1, under 60 is 2, under 128 is 3, else 4.

Suggestions: suggest(pw) returns exactly these strings, only when they apply, always in this order: "use at least 12 characters" (len < 12); "add uppercase letters", "add lowercase letters", "add digits", "add symbols" (for each missing class); "avoid sequences like 'abcd' or '1234'"; "avoid repeating the same character"; "avoid keyboard runs like 'qwerty'"; "don't end the password with a year" (each when its pattern fires). A genuinely good password gets [].

And one bundle for the API endpoint: evaluate(pw) -> dict with keys bits (the effective bits), score, verdict (reuse the existing labels), patterns, suggestions.

test_strength.py is the acceptance contract, run with python3 test_strength.py — leave the tests alone.
